setting.note: Birahima, a foul-mouthed ten-year-old orphan from the Malinké country of northern Côte d'Ivoire, sets out to find his aunt and is swept into the 1990s civil wars of Liberia and Sierra Leone, becoming a child soldier passed from warlord to warlord. Kourouma maps a real and terrible West Africa: the boy's home in the north, the border crossing into war, the warlord camps of Liberia, and the diamond fields of Sierra Leone, all narrated in a child's blunt, dictionary-quoting voice.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ahmadou Kourouma, first published 2000 (Allah n'est pas obligé). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Allah Is Not Obliged, Ahmadou Kourouma (2000). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The blood-brown of the wars Birahima walks through, the horror his child's voice reports as though it were the most ordinary thing in the world. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
